text: June 30 in the History of Computer Science: on this day in 1948, Bell
Labs publicly unveiled the transistor in New York City. Developed by Bardeen, Brattain,
and Shockley, this tiny semiconductor device offered a reliable alternative to bulky vacuum tubes.
Its introduction launched a new era in electronics, enabling smaller, more efficient radios
and computers. The transistor soon became the foundation for integrated circuits and modern computing.
